title: Program Manager, Mapping Operations
description: Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states. What if your work contributed to a better future for mobility, while saving thousands of lives? At Waymo, our teams are made up of curious people who come together from diverse backgrounds to make tangible progress toward our goals. We’re big enough to give our teams the support they need and small enough that every person makes a big difference. There are still a lot of hard problems to solve, and we encourage everyone to chase whatever challenge motivates them. Join us as we bring the Waymo Driver to the world.  As the Mapping Operations team scales, the demand for Mapping subject matter experts also increases. We need smart, motivated Program Managers who can build up technical knowledge in one or more functional areas of Mapping. You will be the primary point of contact for coordinating process and tooling changes with engineering and vendor teams, drafting documentation, piloting new tools/processes, resolving escalations, and identifying areas for streamlining our map build workflows. This role will play a critical part ensuring we continue to build and maintain high quality map in a cost efficient manner. You will report to a Program Manager in this role. You will: Develop deep subject matter expertise in a vertical of mapping operations, and serve as a go-to point of contact for various internal teams Test and roll out new mapping tools, features, and workflows in coordination with our Engineering partners Create, maintain and own the Policy documentation, Content Management and Process Change Management pipeline Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and quality, and own those solutions end-to-end You have: A Bachelor’s degree in a technical or business discipline 4+ years of relevant experience in technical operations or mapping 2+ years of relevant experience in SOP and policy documentation, technical writing or content management Curiosity and ability to learn new tools and workflows quickly Excellent written and verbal communication skills  We prefer: Technical documentation, Knowledge/Content lifecycle management and training content or delivery experience Worked in mapping, GIS, ride-sharing, or logistics Supported or managed large vendor operations teams Experience with GenAI based tools for Process documentation and Knowledge management  Travel requirements : Ability to travel to the US as needed The expected base salary range for this full-time position is listed below. Actual starting pay will be based on job-related factors, including exact work location, experience, relevant training and education, and skill level.  Waymo employees are also eligible to participate in Waymo’s discretionary annual bonus program, equity incentive plan, and generous Company benefits program, subject to eligibility requirements. Salary Range ₹1,875,000 — ₹2,230,000 INR
